"The art of medicine consists of amusing the patient while nature cures the disease." — Voltaire, attributed
"To do two things at once is to do neither." — Publilius Syrus, Maxims
"Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood." — Marie Curie, attributed
"The beginning of wisdom is the definition of terms." — Socrates, attributed
"We shall not cease from exploration, and the end of all our exploring will be to arrive where we started and know the place for the first time." — T.S. Eliot, Little Gidding
"Do not go where the path may lead; go instead where there is no path and leave a trail." — Ralph Waldo Emerson, Self-Reliance
"The cure for anything is salt water — sweat, tears, or the sea." — Isak Dinesen, Seven Gothic Tales
"In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity." — Albert Einstein, attributed
"First, do no harm." — Hippocrates, Epidemics
"The greatest wealth is health." — Virgil, attributed
"Nature itself is the best physician." — Hippocrates, Aphorisms
"Wherever the art of medicine is loved, there is also a love of humanity." — Hippocrates, attributed
"I am not afraid of storms, for I am learning how to sail my ship." — Louisa May Alcott, Little Women
"The only way to do great work is to love what you do." — Steve Jobs, Stanford Commencement 2005
"Attention is the rarest and purest form of generosity." — Simone Weil, First and Last Notebooks
"The secret of getting ahead is getting started." — Mark Twain, attributed
"It is not enough to be busy; so are the ants. The question is: what are we busy about?" — Henry David Thoreau, Life Without Principle
"Out of your vulnerabilities will come your strength." — Sigmund Freud, attributed
"The good physician treats the disease; the great physician treats the patient who has the disease." — William Osler, Aequanimitas
"Science is organized knowledge. Wisdom is organized life." — Immanuel Kant, attributed
"The most important thing in communication is hearing what isn't said." — Peter Drucker, The Effective Executive
"To know what you know and what you do not know, that is true knowledge." — Confucius, Analects
"Excellence is never an accident. It is always the result of high intention, sincere effort, and intelligent execution." — Aristotle, attributed
"The best preparation for tomorrow is doing your best today." — H. Jackson Brown Jr., Life's Little Instruction Book
"Time is the coin of your life. Be careful lest you let other people spend it for you." — Carl Sandburg, attributed
"The function of the human body is to carry the brain around." — Thomas Edison, attributed
"In character, in manner, in style, in all things, the supreme excellence is simplicity." — Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, attributed
"That which does not kill us makes us stronger." — Friedrich Nietzsche, Twilight of the Idols
"The quality of a person's life is in direct proportion to their commitment to excellence." — Vince Lombardi, attributed
"He who has health has hope, and he who has hope has everything." — Arabian proverb
"The whole secret of a successful life is to find out what is one's destiny to do, and then do it." — Henry Ford, attributed
"Not everything that can be counted counts, and not everything that counts can be counted." — William Bruce Cameron, Informal Sociology
"Medicine is not only a science; it is also an art." — Paracelsus, attributed
"The physician's highest calling is to make sick people healthy." — Samuel Hahnemann, Organon of Medicine
"A good laugh and a long sleep are the best cures in the doctor's book." — Irish proverb
"The human body is the best picture of the human soul." — Ludwig Wittgenstein, Philosophical Investigations
"Knowing is not enough; we must apply. Willing is not enough; we must do." — Johann Wolfgang von Goethe, attributed
"The secret of change is to focus all of your energy not on fighting the old, but on building the new." — Socrates, attributed via Dan Millman
"I would rather have questions that cannot be answered than answers that cannot be questioned." — Richard Feynman, attributed
"Every patient carries her or his own doctor inside." — Albert Schweitzer, attributed
"The measure of intelligence is the ability to change." — Albert Einstein, attributed
"Health is not valued till sickness comes." — Thomas Fuller, Gnomologia
"Wherever you go, go with all your heart." — Confucius, Analects
"The greatest mistake in the treatment of diseases is that there are physicians for the body and physicians for the soul, although the two cannot be separated." — Plato, Charmides
"Without continual growth and progress, such words as improvement, achievement, and success have no meaning." — Benjamin Franklin, Poor Richard's Almanack
"We know what we are, but know not what we may be." — William Shakespeare, Hamlet
"It is health that is real wealth, and not pieces of gold and silver." — Mahatma Gandhi, attributed
"The art is long, life is short, opportunity fleeting, experience treacherous, judgment difficult." — Hippocrates, Aphorisms
"What we learn with pleasure we never forget." — Alfred Mercier, attributed
"The greatest medicine of all is teaching people how not to need it." — Hippocrates, attributed
"Real generosity toward the future lies in giving all to the present." — Albert Camus, The Rebel
"The secret of success is constancy of purpose." — Benjamin Disraeli, Speech 1872
"A word of kindness is seldom spoken in vain, while witty sayings are as easily lost as the pearls slipping from a broken string." — George Prentice, Prenticeana
